spec_match.rationale: Exercises ::first-letter, ::first-line, ::selection, :hover::before, :hover::after, ::before, and ::after chains on a real shadow-DOM card.

interactive.rationale: Four theme buttons still swap host-page ::part() selector sets and update the selector log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page continues to deliver the feature-index promise of theme swapping with newly unblocked ::part() chains.

edge_cases.rationale: The pre-130 labels are still based on a static heuristic and no theme intentionally exercises a selector that remains rejected.

references.rationale: The see-also section now includes the mandatory ChromeStatus link for feature 5195333643272192.

design_system.rationale: The page styling remains token-based with contrast-safe theme button states.

openQuestions.title: Replace the regex-based pre-130 heuristic with real CSS.supports() probes
openQuestions.detail: preChrome130Accepts() approximates the old grammar with a regex; this is fine for show but the page already runs in Chrome 130+ where the new chains succeed. Hard-coding a static expectation table per selector or comparing against a known reference list of pre-130-allowed pseudos would be more accurate than parsing strings in JS.

openQuestions.title: Add a fifth theme that exercises a still-rejected selector
openQuestions.detail: All four themes use only newly-OK chains. A fifth 'experimental' theme that intentionally includes a combinator (::part(card) > .x) or :has() would show what the spec still forbids and reinforce the boundary.

summary: The theme swap demo remains a strong real ::part() composition showcase, and now includes the required ChromeStatus self-heal anchor.
